message:
fix(tools): eliminate ripgrep reader thread race in GrepTool

Fix a race condition where fast ripgrep executions could exit before
the stdout reader thread delivered all JSON match lines to the channel,
causing false "No matches found" results.

Root cause: the main thread called child.wait() and then immediately
tried to receive from stdout_rx, but the reader thread (spawned via
std::thread::spawn) might not have finished pushing all parsed lines
through the channel yet.

Fix:
- Capture thread handles from std::thread::spawn (stdout_thread,
  stderr_thread) instead of discarding them
- After child.wait() or child.kill(), join both reader threads to
  ensure all pipe data is consumed before reading from channels
- Drain remaining stdout_rx and stderr_rx messages after thread join
- Construct stderr_text only after join completes
- Extract process_rg_json_match_line() as a standalone function from
  the inline closure for clarity and testability

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
